title: Barriers to data access and matching in Europe
summary_en: Chapter in the Castellani-Koch volume *Mapping competitiveness with European data*. Surveys the European firm-level data landscape and documents the practical and legal barriers to linking national firm registers across countries. The Hungarian case is featured as one of the better-organised national datasets, but cross-border matching remains painful.
policy_relevance: Useful for KSH and Eurostat: the lack of linked firm data is a binding constraint on EU-wide competitiveness analysis. Supports the case for legal and technical investment in cross-border microdata access (CompNet, EuropeanDataLab). Caveat: written before GDPR, so the legal landscape has shifted.
